_comment: Cartopian PM containment — Tier-2 native-sandbox DEPTH profile for the gemini harness (FR-007 / TASK-03-002). The Cartopian source of truth, loaded by wrappers/bin/cartopian-gemini-pm, that drives gemini's OWN native OS sandbox BENEATH the DEC-001 tool-removal floor. gemini's native sandbox is Apple Seatbelt (sandbox-exec) on macOS and a container / gVisor runtime on Linux; this profile selects gemini's per-TOOL sandbox (settings key security.toolSandboxing=true, which isolates individual tool executions) plus the macOS SEATBELT_PROFILE that confines tool writes to the isolated launch surface. The floor (tools.exclude removes run_shell_command/write_file/read_file/replace/web_*/read_mcp_resource/etc.) already removes every built-in filesystem/exec/web tool, so this layer is normally inert; it exists for defense-in-depth: if the floor were ever bypassed and a write/shell tool reappeared, gemini's native sandbox + workspace boundary still deny the product-repo / work-root WRITE reach and process-exec effects. Config + native mechanism only; no third-party / bundled sandbox (NF-001). Absolute paths are host/project-specific, exactly as wrappers/etc/mcp-cartopian-only.json hard-codes the cartopian-mcp path; regenerate them for another project root / work root.
mechanism: gemini native sandbox: Apple Seatbelt (sandbox-exec) on macOS, Docker/Podman/gVisor(runsc) on Linux. This profile uses gemini's PER-TOOL sandbox (security.toolSandboxing=true) — it isolates each built-in tool execution rather than re-exec'ing the whole gemini process, so the model's tool executions are confined while gemini's own API connection AND the out-of-process Cartopian MCP server stay functional. The macOS seatbelt policy is selected by SEATBELT_PROFILE (built-in .sb profiles shipped with gemini-cli); writes are confined to the workspace (launch cwd) + temp. Native mechanism + config only; no bundled sandbox (NF-001).

gemini_settings._note: The exact gemini settings keys the floor wrapper applies for the depth layer. security.toolSandboxing=true turns on gemini's per-tool OS sandbox so a (hypothetical, post-floor-bypass) reappeared built-in tool runs under Seatbelt confined to the workspace; gemini's workspace boundary independently denies write_file targets outside the launch cwd (captured: a write_file to the product repo is refused 'outside my allowed workspace directories', no file on disk). The launch cwd is the isolated PM surface (NOT the product repo, NOT a work root), so the workspace = the surface and product-repo / work-root writes are denied. SEATBELT_PROFILE=restrictive-open applies strict write restrictions while leaving gemini's own outbound API reachable (a 'proxied'/whole-process variant would starve gemini's API and the MCP child — see whole_process_note).

whole_process_note: gemini ALSO offers a whole-process sandbox (-s / --sandbox / GEMINI_SANDBOX=sandbox-exec) that re-execs the ENTIRE gemini process under Seatbelt. This profile deliberately does NOT use it: the whole-process sandbox also confines gemini's own model-API network call and the stdio Cartopian MCP server it spawns as a child, so under a write-restricting/proxied profile gemini's API is starved (captured: a whole-process restrictive-proxied run times out at exit 144) and the MCP server cannot mediate the project. The per-tool sandbox (security.toolSandboxing) is the correct depth mechanism — it isolates tool executions only, exactly as codex's per-command Seatbelt and Claude Code's tool-level sandbox keep the out-of-process MCP server exempt.
mcp_server_note: gemini launches the cartopian MCP server (wrappers/etc/mcp-cartopian-only.json command) as a long-lived stdio subprocess at session start, OUTSIDE the per-tool sandbox that wraps model-driven built-in tool executions. The MCP server therefore retains its own filesystem access and the contained PM stays functional through the Cartopian toolset — exactly as the Claude Code and codex floors rely on out-of-process MCP access (captured: cartopian discover_projects returns OK under toolSandboxing=true).
read_note: Unlike codex, gemini's built-in MCP-resource read tools (list_mcp_resources / read_mcp_resource) ARE removable from the model's surface via tools.exclude (captured: with them excluded the contained PM reports NO_READ_TOOL and cannot reach a cross-project resource; with them present it read a project's REQUIREMENTS resource). gemini therefore reaches a genuine no-read-tool floor and carries NO read forcing residual.
web_note: Unlike codex's server-side web_search, gemini's web tools (web_fetch, google_web_search) are CLIENT-side built-ins removed by tools.exclude at the floor (captured: NO_WEB_TOOL). gemini therefore carries NO web/browse forcing residual.
